**Marco Ferrando** (born 18 July 1954, [Genoa](/wiki/Genoa "Genoa")) is an Italian [Trotskyist](/wiki/Trotskyist "Trotskyist") activist and politician, a leading member of the [Workers' Communist Party](/wiki/Workers%27_Communist_Party_(Italy) "Workers' Communist Party (Italy)"), Italian section of the [International Socialist League (2019)](/wiki/International_Socialist_League_(2019) "International Socialist League (2019)").

In February 2006, in the build-up to the [2006 Italian general election](/wiki/2006_Italian_general_election "2006 Italian general election"), he was removed from the [Communist Refoundation Party](/wiki/Communist_Refoundation_Party "Communist Refoundation Party") (PRC)'s list of candidates for the [Senate of Italy](/wiki/Senate_of_Italy "Senate of Italy") because of his support for the right of Iraqis to [resist occupation](/wiki/Right_to_resist_occupation "Right to resist occupation"), and criticism of [Zionism](/wiki/Zionism "Zionism").

When the PRC joined the current center-left government, the [AMR Progetto Comunista](/wiki/AMR_Progetto_Comunista "AMR Progetto Comunista") left the PRC. After that, they dissolved and formed the [Movement for the Communist Workers' Party](/wiki/Movement_for_the_Communist_Workers%27_Party "Movement for the Communist Workers' Party").

He is an [atheist](/wiki/Atheist "Atheist").[[1]](#cite_note-1)
